title: Cache negative results against penetration
description: Store not-found answers briefly so repeated absent-key lookups do not penetrate to the datastore.
body: Use when lookups for nonexistent keys repeatedly reach an expensive origin. Do not leave misses uncached when a hot path or attacker can request many absent keys — each one penetrates straight through to the datastore. Actions: cache the negative result (null/404) with a short TTL, or front lookups with a Bloom filter that cheaply rejects impossible keys. Validation signals include origin miss traffic dropping for known-absent keys. Risk boundary: a too-long negative TTL hides a key that is later created — keep negative TTLs short and invalidate them on write.
